36530915d191495cd8906c973edafe96b7c1997c: “Birds are afraid of curved branches”: The invisible resistance of the stock market
66bf8dce2b48b88fe7962ca8f54f3459126d2ae4: The article emphasizes that the "birds are afraid of bent branches" mentality is the major drag on the stock market, when geopolitical or policy shocks often only act as triggers for sell-offs. In March, VN-Index decreased in 14/21 sessions, on March 9 alone it lost more than 115 points, equivalent to 6.51%, in the context of Brent oil rising to 111 USD/barrel. A similar shock was the April 3 session, when the VN-Index dropped nearly 88 points, equivalent to 6.68%, after news that the US imposed a 46% reciprocal tax on Vietnam, then recovered when there was news of a 90-day tax delay. With individual investors accounting for more than 80% of transactions, the market easily falls into a cycle of sell-offs, technical pullbacks, FOMO and then losses, causing many people to buy at the top and sell at the bottom. The overall impact is negative in the short term on market sentiment and liquidity, but there are no specific stocks in the article to evaluate separately.

Approaching Basel III standards with new ratios The most important highlight in this draft is the addition of liquidity and capital management ratios according to Basel III standards. Specifically: Solvency ratio (LCR): The draft introduces the LCR ratio to ensure banks have enough high liquid assets to meet payment needs in a 30-day stress scenario. 446a2282e47e46268f73b9ee5852ce5c96be45bb: In the first quarter of 2026, IDC recorded net revenue of 1,485 billion VND and net profit of 270 billion VND, down 17% and 21% YoY, respectively, the lowest level in 10 quarters. The main reason is fluctuations in revenue from industrial park infrastructure leasing contracts that are eligible for one-time recognition, causing this period's profit to decline. IDC has just completed 17% of the revenue plan and 15% of the pre-tax profit plan for 2026, while the full year target is revenue of 9,474 billion VND and profit before tax of more than 2.9 trillion VND. At the end of March 2026, IDC had total assets of more than 23.5 trillion VND, bank deposits of more than 7.5 trillion VND, debt of more than 5.7 trillion VND and unrealized revenue/advance from buyers of nearly 6.3 trillion VND. Short-term information is negative for IDC shares due to reduced profits and low progress in completing the plan, but the large amount of deposits and the plan to lease 100 hectares of industrial park land and develop more than 100.4 thousand m2 of factories are still medium-term supports.
